title: ActiveCampaign vs Campaign Monitor (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare ActiveCampaign and Campaign Monitor. See pricing, features, ratings, and which email marketing tool wins for your business needs.
h1: ActiveCampaign vs Campaign Monitor: Detailed Feature & Pricing Comparison
intro: Both ActiveCampaign and Campaign Monitor are established email marketing platforms, but they serve different business needs. ActiveCampaign emphasizes automation and CRM capabilities, while Campaign Monitor focuses on design and multi-brand management. This comparison will help you determine which tool aligns best with your marketing strategy.
choose_a_if: You need advanced marketing automation and lead scoring capabilities
You want an integrated CRM system with your email marketing platform
You plan to use SMS marketing alongside email campaigns
choose_b_if: You manage multiple brands or client accounts and need white-label solutions
Design and template flexibility are your top priorities
You're looking for the most budget-friendly option to start

faq.q: Is ActiveCampaign better than Campaign Monitor?
faq.a: It depends on your needs. ActiveCampaign excels in automation, CRM, and lead scoring, making it better for businesses needing sophisticated marketing workflows. Campaign Monitor is better suited for agencies managing multiple brands with a focus on design and ease of use. ActiveCampaign has higher G2 (4.5) and Trustpilot (4.1) ratings overall.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, ActiveCampaign or Campaign Monitor?
faq.a: Campaign Monitor starts at $11/month compared to ActiveCampaign's $15/month, making it the more affordable initial option. However, pricing scales differently for each platform based on subscriber count, so the overall cost difference depends on your list size and feature needs.

faq.q: Can I switch from Campaign Monitor to ActiveCampaign?
faq.a: Yes, most email marketing platforms allow data migration between services. Both tools support importing contacts and subscriber lists, though you may need to manually recreate campaigns and automations. It's recommended to test ActiveCampaign's features with a small segment before fully migrating.
